title: Reviewer→implementer handoff record (review-feedback)
description: Single JSONL record appended to .scratch/handoff.jsonl by one of the four reviewer agents after a review pass. Carries structured findings that the implementer can act on directly — the schema converts reviewer narratives into discrete, prescriptive items so the implementer-side rework loop processes diffs, not paragraphs.

author.description: Which reviewer produced the feedback. The four reviewer agents map one-to-one onto this enum.

verdict.description: approved → no findings required, feature can proceed if all four reviewers concur; changes_requested → implementer fixes findings then re-runs the gate; blocked → critical issue, must resolve before merge.

findings.description: List of discrete review findings. May be empty when verdict is "approved".

tag.description: How the finding should be processed. autofix → implementer fixes immediately; blocked → must fix before merge; escalate → human decision in escalations file; clarify → ask the named agent for clarification.

location.description: Where the finding applies. Ideally `path:line` (e.g. `src/main/java/com/example/Foo.java:45`); a path alone is acceptable when the finding is file-scope.

description.description: What the finding is. Plain prose, one or two sentences.

fix.description: Concrete suggested fix. Strongly recommended for tag=autofix; converts implementer rework from paragraph-parsing to applying a diff.

clarify_target.description: Required when tag is "clarify". Names the agent the implementer should consult.

severity.description: Optional severity tag. Use "critical" for items that must block merge; "fixable" for issues that can be addressed in a follow-up.

bar_clause.description: Optional pointer to the conjunctive-bar clause this finding violates. Canonical slug list and per-reviewer mapping in .claude/skills/review-checklist/SKILL.md § Quality-Bar Clause Mapping; clauses themselves are defined in docs/tdd-principles.md §§ Scope Discipline / Code That Reads Cold / Operationally Honest. feature-eval uses this to score bar adherence mechanically. Reviewers should set this whenever a finding maps to a clause; omit when the finding is procedural (typo, lint, etc.) and does not target a clause.

recommendations.description: Non-blocking suggestions the reviewer offers. Optional.

approved_aspects.description: What passed review cleanly. Optional. Useful diagnostic when only one reviewer rejects.
